bids.reasoning: At the opening (t=0.0), I am targeting my maximum-utility bid because there is no time pressure to concede yet. There are no opponent offers or messages to infer their priorities, so I will start with my best values on all issues, including issueD where I am indifferent due to zero weight.

At t=0.02 it is very early, so I am targeting a near-maximal utility bid rather than making substantive concessions yet. The opponent’s first offer gives me only moderate utility and mostly concedes issueE while pushing against my highest-weight issueF, so I will hold firm on my important issues while conceding issueD, which has zero weight for me and matches their signaled preference.

I am targeting my maximum-utility bid because it is still very early at t=0.04 and there is no need to make meaningful concessions yet. The opponent’s offers give me low utility and have not improved for me except on issueD, which has zero importance to me, so their cooperative messages do not justify conceding on high-weight issues like issueF, issueB, or issueE.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.966, since it is still very early at t=0.06 and the opponent’s offers have not improved for me. Their messages are cooperative but cheap talk; their bids consistently ask me to give up high-value issueF and issueB, so I am holding those while conceding only on lower-cost issueA and indifferent issueD.

At t=0.08 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making concessions on low/zero-importance issues. The opponent’s offers consistently demand issueE=valueA, which is already best for me, and they seem to push issueA/issueD while holding issueB, issueC, and issueF away from my preferences; I will concede issueA and issueD but protect the high-weight issueF and important issueB/issueE. Their messages are cooperative but do not show real numerical concession, so I am not making major concessions yet.

At t=0.10 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making visible concessions on lower-importance issues rather than giving up on issueF, issueB, or issueE. The opponent’s offers consistently give me issueE:valueA but stay poor for issueF and issueC, and their cooperative messages are not backed by much numerical concession, so I will hold firm on my most valuable issues while varying issueA/issueD to search for overlap.

At t=0.12 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid for me while making concessions on lower-weight issues. The opponent’s offers consistently give me issueE:valueA and have recently improved issueF to valueB, but they still ask for weak outcomes on my high-weight issueF and issueB, so I hold those at my best values while conceding issueA, issueC, and issueD.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.81 because it is still early at t=0.14, so there is no need to make major concessions on my most important issues, especially issueF, issueE, and issueB. The opponent’s bids remain low-value for me and show only limited concession, while their messages are cooperative but not backed by substantial numerical movement. I concede on lower-weight issues like issueA, issueC, and issueD to improve acceptability without giving up my key value drivers.

I am targeting a utility around 0.79, which is still strong for us at the early time fraction of 0.16 while making visible concessions on lower-weight issues. The opponent’s offers consistently push issueC and issueD toward valueA and largely keep issueE at valueA, so I am conceding on issueA/issueC/D while holding firm on our high-importance issueB and issueF.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.74 while making visible concessions on lower-weight issues issueA, issueC, and costless issueD. At t=0.18 it is still early, so I am holding firm on my most important issues, especially issueF and issueB, while matching parts of the opponent’s latest pattern such as issueA=valueB, issueC=valueA, issueD=valueB, and issueE=valueA. The opponent’s messages are cooperative but not binding; their offers show only modest concession, mainly on issueF moving to valueB, so I am not yet dropping my high-weight demands further.

At t=0.20 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid for myself while making modest concessions on lower-weight issues. The opponent’s actual offers mostly keep issueE at valueA, which is good for me, but they have not meaningfully conceded on my most important issueF or issueB; their cooperative messages are cheap talk compared with the low utility of their bids to me. I will hold firm on issueF, issueB, and issueE, while varying lower-weight issueA/issueC and irrelevant issueD to search for agreement without repeating my last bid.

At t=0.22 it is still early enough to target a strong utility for me while showing movement on lower-weight issues. The opponent’s offers consistently push issueC toward valueA and avoid issueB/issueF valueC, but issueB and especially issueF are high-weight for me, so I am holding those while conceding on issueA/issueC and the zero-weight issueD. Their messages are cooperative but mostly cheap talk; the actual offers remain low utility to me, so I am not making a large concession yet.

I am targeting a strong but not maximal utility bid (about 0.814), preserving my highest-weight priorities on issueF, issueB, and issueE while conceding on lower-weight issueA and issueC and giving issueD freely since it has zero weight for me. At t=0.24 there is still time, so I should not drop to the opponent’s low-utility bids yet; their offers show some movement on issueC/issueF but continued resistance to issueB=valueC and issueF=valueC, while their partnership messages are cheap talk rather than binding constraints.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around the upper-mid range by holding firm on my highest-weight issues issueF, issueB, and issueE, while conceding on lower-weight issueA, issueC, and issueD. At t=0.26 there is no need for a major late-stage concession yet, and the opponent’s offers remain low for me despite repeated cooperative messages, so I trust the bid pattern more than the emotional appeals.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.79 by holding firm on my most important issues, especially issueF, issueB, and issueE, while conceding on lower-weight issues like issueC and issueD. At t=0.28 there is still time, so I should not make major concessions on high-weight issues yet; the opponent’s offers remain low for me and their messages are mostly partnership appeals rather than evidence of real movement. Their history suggests they often want issueC:valueA and issueD:valueA, so I can offer those without sacrificing much.

I am targeting a strong but not maximal bid (about 0.81 utility to me), since we are still early-mid at t=0.30 and there is no need to concede heavily on my most important issues. The opponent’s offers consistently push issueF away from my preferred value and rarely offer issueB:valueC, so I hold firm on those high-weight issues while conceding on lower-weight issueA and issueC and matching their recent issueD preference, which costs me nothing. Their messages are cooperative/emotional appeals, but the numerical offers show only limited concession, so I should rely on the bidding pattern rather than the wording.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.81 for me, since it is still only t=0.32 and there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent’s offers remain low for my utility and their messages are mostly emotional appeals, but their bids suggest they may tolerate concessions on low-weight issues like issueA, issueC, and issueD, while I should hold firm on issueB, issueE, and especially issueF.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.79 because we are only at t=0.34, so I should concede on lower-weight issues before giving up my high-weight priorities. The opponent’s offers consistently push issueA/issueC toward valueA and never give issueB or issueF at my best values, so I am yielding on issueA, issueC, and the zero-weight issueD while holding issueB, issueE, and issueF. Their messages are cooperative but mostly emotional cheap talk, so I rely primarily on the bid pattern.

I am targeting about 0.74 utility: I concede on lower-weight issues issueA, issueC, and issueD in line with the opponent’s recent bids, while holding my most important issues issueF, issueE, and issueB at strong values. At t=0.36 there is some pressure to reward the opponent’s latest concession on issueF, but it is still too early to drop major weighted issues unnecessarily. The opponent’s messages are cooperative but repetitive cheap talk; the actual offers show they have only recently moved meaningfully on issueF while still pushing against my preferences on issueB, issueC, and issueE.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.68 while making a meaningful mid-game concession on issueB and the lower-weight issueA/issueC. The opponent’s offers remain low for me overall, but they have signaled possible flexibility on issueF once and often allow issueE: valueA, so I will hold firm on high-value issueF and issueE while moving toward them elsewhere.
